title: The Year of the Rose by Algernon Charles Swinburne — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
From the depths of the green garden-closes
Where the summer in darkness dozes
Till autumn pluck from his hand
An hour-glass that holds not a sand;
From the maze that a flower-belt encloses
To the stones and sea-grass on the strand
How red was the reign of the roses
Over the rose-crowned land!
meaning: "The Year of the Rose" by Algernon Charles Swinburne is a sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 94 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "From the depths of the green garden-closes Where the summer in darkness dozes...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Algernon Charles Swinburne's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
